title: alkekengi procumbens
intro: Alkekengi procumbens, also known as Physalis alkekengi or Chinese lantern, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia, characterized by its bright red, papery husks that contain orange berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its seeds, leaves, and roots exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Alkekengi procumbens is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting digestive health. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
